description: Two different doctor appointments in same week - should NOT match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Dermatology Appointment
- Start: 2026-06-04T14:00:00
- End: 2026-06-04T14:30:00
- Location: Skin Health Clinic, Suite 301
- Description: Annual skin check with Dr. Patel

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Dentist - Dr. Lee
   - Start: 2026-06-02T09:00:00
   - End: 2026-06-02T10:00:00
   - Location: Bright Smiles Dental
   - Description: 6-month cleaning and checkup

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: Eye Exam
   - Start: 2026-06-05T11:00:00
   - End: 2026-06-05T12:00:00
   - Location: Vision Care Center
   - Description: Annual eye examination with Dr. Wong

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Team Lunch
   - Start: 2026-06-04T12:00:00
   - End: 2026-06-04T13:00:00
   - Location: Italian Garden
   - Description: Monthly team lunch

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: {"matched_event_id":null,"reasoning":"No match. The new Dermatology Appointment is on 2026-06-04 14:00–14:30 at Skin Health Clinic; the only existing event on 2026-06-04 is Team Lunch (12:00–13:00 at Italian Garden) which differs in time, title, location and purpose. Other events are on different dates."}
